feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Integration Suite
feed.subtitle: SAP Integration Suite Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: HTTPS Sender adapter in iFlow shows sometimes a 401 error
entry.content:
Hi

I have an iFlow with a HTTPS sender adapter. We created a specific role and assigned the role to this iFlow and a clientID (generated in BTP). In general everything is working fine. An external partner is calling the endpoint and sending messages (XML IDOCs).

However we now have a situation that a specific message (relatively large, size 1MB, XML IDOC) is not being processed by the HTTPS sender adapter.

When checking the Integration Suite Logs I see the following that a HTTP 401 is returned.

Also a successfull request is having 401s but will get a 200 in a next request and will continue.

How do I need to interpret this and how can this be solved?

Thanks

Ron

entry.title: Integration Suite Event Mesh Capability - Programmatically query queues
entry.content:
Hello,

I'd like to know if there is a programmatic way to query queues (names, Queue Size, number of Messages Queued, number of Unacknowledged Messages) like by using an ODATA service.

We need to create an alert if messages stored in the queues are not being consumed by the iFlows, but right now, we couldn't find a way to do it, only a manual way.

Thanks

entry.title: SAP S/4 HANA Public CLoud - send SalesOrder (SAP_COM_0109) to Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I would currently like to establish a technical connection between the SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud and Cloud Integration. I would like to distribute SalesOrder information (SAP_COM_0109 - Service Sales Order (A2X)) to the cloud integration via an OData adapter. In the SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud, I have made settings for Communication Arrangements, Communication Systems and Maintain Communication Users. How do I manually trigger a SalesOrder message from the S/4 HANA Public Cloud, which steps do I have to perform? What does the endpoint in the OData (EDMX file I pulled from “https://my420450-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_SALES_ORDER_SRV/$metadata”) adapter look like as well as the entity set (currently A_SalesOrder)?

Thanks in advance and best regards

Michael Seydler

entry.title: HTTP response '502: Bad Gateway. Unable to open connection to backend system'
entry.content:
Get the below error while connecting from SAP CPI iflow to backend ERP via cloud connector.

HTTP response '502: Bad Gateway. Unable to open connection to backend system' http://wa2xxxx.xxx.com:8443/sap/bc/srt/idoc?sap-client=300

URL is 'reachable' in BTP cockpit.

It works in QA but does not work in PRD.

Thanks

entry.title: Fetch Compensation Design Worksheet Fields via Odata API Entity
entry.content:
Hi Community,

I have got one requirement to fetch the Compensation design worksheet fields via OData API entity. Is there any entity to fetch the Compensation design worksheet fields.

 

Best Regards,

Pankaj

 

#SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central #SAP SuccessFactors Compensation #SAP Integration Suite

entry.title: Reading a property in Content Filter
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I am trying to read a property in Content Filter expression of SAP CPI. Mentioned below is the nodelist Xpath expression. But I am getting error at this expression. Could you please assist to correct this nodelist expression.

/OnboardingCandidateInfo/OnboardingCandidateInfo[(fName/text() =function:simple('${property.fName}'))]

 

Error at Content Filter

java.lang.NullPointerException: Cannot invoke "net.sf.saxon.expr.oper.OperandArray.getNumberOfOperands()" because the return value of "net.sf.saxon.expr.FunctionCall.getOperanda()" is null

 

Regards,

Varun Vichare.

entry.title: Event Mesh IS Subscribe Access Denied
entry.content:
Hi, I'm trying to consume messages from a  SAP Event Mesh Integration Suite queue via HTTP. The exact same setup worked perfectly fine a few days ago.

Publishing via the HTTP endpoint (/messagingrest/v1/queues/{queue-name}/messages) works. But consuming the same message via the HTTP endpoint (/messagingrest/v1/queues/{queue-name}/messages/consumption) returns the following response:

{
    "error": {
        "code": "403",
        "message": "subscribe access denied"
    },
    "message": "subscribe access denied"
}

I tried creating a whole new EM instance + service key + queue (with/without namespace) + topic. Nothing seems to work.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: Download sales order attachments as a physical file (pdf,...)
entry.content:
Hello all
I have a requirement to download sales order attachments as a physical file (pdf,...). Is there a BAPI or API that can be used to do this. We will be doing it in CPI. Does anyone have any experience with this?

entry.title: IDOC metadata change on SAP backend - Cloud Integration
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are working on IDOC to IDOC scenario on BTP Cloud Integration(CI) where our receiver system is also another CI which is on another subaccount ...IDOC metadata has been changed on SAP backend.. we need to adapt the IDOC schema changes on Cloud Integration to synchronize the schema on both SAP Backend & CI.. should we ask SAP backend colleagues to share XSD (or) WSDL? which is better practice) if they share XSD/WSDL then should we also the share the same with receiver BTP CI system contact? 

Thanks,

Ramu.

entry.title: How to concatenate value in Global Variable
entry.content:
1. In data from Payment, I have below data get from Odata.

<A_OperationalAcctgDocItemCube>

<A_OperationalAcctgDocItemCubeType>

<FiscalYear>2025</FiscalYear>

<Customer>15100100</Customer>

<PostingDate>2025-02-06T00:00:00.000</PostingDate>

<AmountInTransactionCurrency>40000</AmountInTransactionCurrency>

<AccountingDocument>1800000006</AccountingDocument>

<DocumentDate>2025-02-08T00:00:00.000</DocumentDate>

<CompanyCode>1510</CompanyCode>

<AccountingDocumentItem>1</AccountingDocumentItem>

<TransactionCurrency>JPY</TransactionCurrency>

</A_OperationalAcctgDocItemCubeType>

</A_OperationalAcctgDocItemCube>

2. I use General Splitter for the result of data as I want to work with each record of "A_OperationalAcctgDocItemCubeType".

3. In Assign Invoice data, I get the AccountingDocument and AmountInTransactionCurrency and assign the value to the Property variable.

4. I call another odata by using the filter value of AccountingDocument get in step-3.

5. I use Write Variable to store the data.

6. I use Router and if there is data in Step-5, it will goes to next step and if no data, it will end.

7. I used Groovy script to update the Variable declared in Step-5. Below is my groovy script. I want to concatenate the InvoiceID used in step-5 with the InvoiceID get from Property.

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;

def Message processData(Message message) {

    def currentPartialID = message.getProperty("InvoiceID")

    def existingIDs = message.getProperty("PartialInvoiceID") ?: ""

    existingIDs = existingIDs.tokenize(',') // Convert string to list

    if (!existingIDs.contains(currentPartialID)) {

        existingIDs.add(currentPartialID) // Append only if new

    }

    message.setProperty("PartialInvoiceID", existingIDs.join(',')) // Convert list back to string

    return message;

}

 

But when I run my process, the PartialInvoiceID is not concatenate with the InvoiceID and it resets the value when one splitter is finished. And assign only one value.

Is there any way that I can concatenate the PartialInvoiceID?

E.g 
What I want
After 1st Splitter > PartialInvoiceID = 1400000006
After 2nd Splitter > PartialInvoiceID = 1400000006,1400000007
After 3rd Splitter > PartialInvoiceID =  1400000006,1400000007, 1400000008
 
What I get
After 1st Splitter > PartialInvoiceID = 1400000006
After 2nd Splitter > PartialInvoiceID = 1400000007
After 3rd Splitter > PartialInvoiceID =  1400000008

entry.title: Retry option in B2B Monitoring getting failed
entry.content:
Hello all,

I'm facing difficulty in retrying failed messages in B2B monitoring. After I trigger the retry option it ends up in Step 3 Receiver Communication Flow failure with error message :

java.lang.NullPointerException: while trying to invoke the method java.lang.String.isEmpty() of a null object loaded from local variable 'address'

Can anyone provide a solution for this issue.

 

Thanks,

Aditya

entry.title: Use Cases of B2B in Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi,

For the B2B module in Integration Suite, what are the use cases for it? In which scenario it is better to use the B2B module vs the normal iFlow? What can be categorized as B2B?

Is the B2B module only suitable for scenarios that are using the known message format such as EDIFACT, etc?

Thank you.

entry.title: OAuth 2.0 SAML Assertion Access Token Generator for SAP SuccessFactors HXM Suite
entry.content:
Dear Experts

I am checking several blog for generate the OAuth 2.0 SAML Assertion Access Token Generator for SAP SuccessFactors HXM Suite.

With the following options in the  blog work fine:

How to use Postman to call SuccessFactors API usin... - SAP Community

Postman to call SuccessFactors API using OAuth aut... - SAP Community

However I have a doubt with this option :

https://github.com/piejanssens/sf-oauth?tab=readme-ov-file#installation 

In the step Generate a new key pair: when I run the command.

$ sf-oauth --newkeypair
...

The response is and I dont know what to do?

 

Regards

entry.title: Advanced Event Mesh for Central contracts
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are implementing Advanced Event Mesh to integrate with our S/4 HANA system for Central Purchase Contracts, following the guidelines from the SAP Business Accelerator Hub blog: https://api.sap.com/event/OP_CENTRALPURCHASECONTRACTEVENTS/resource.

To facilitate this, we have configured the following topics for publishing events related to Central Purchase Contracts:

  • (i) ce/sap/s4/beh/centralpurchasecontract/v1/CentralPurchaseContract/Changed/v1
  • (ii) ce/sap/s4/beh/centralpurchasecontract/v1/CentralPurchaseContract/Created/v1

These topics will receive events corresponding to changes and creation of Central Purchase Contracts, respectively.

We have also created a single queue to subscribe to both of these topics. This setup has been tested successfully by publishing messages to the specified topics using Postman.

Please see below screenshot that shows test is successful from postman and it returns 200 OK status.

 

 

 

Now, we are trying to create channel with topic endpoints on S4HANA backend but we get the following errors while trying to do so.

Would you be able to guide regarding this ? or kindly help me to know if someone already something similar to push the events for S/4HANA?

Please find below screenshots regarding Channel configuration on S/4 HANA backend.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Thanks,

Ramu.

entry.title: SAP Integration suite - OData v2 adapter - error java.lang.NullPointerException
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm using the standard SAP package Integration of Service in SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Field Service Management and have been getting the error java.lang.NullPointerException in Notify Service Order Update from SAP Field Service Management to SAP S4HANA Cloud.

I've checked that it happens at the step 

I haven't made any updates regarding this iFlow so I'm not sure if SAP has updated anything last weekend.

Has anyone seen this error before?

Regards,

Minh

entry.title: CPI iFlow getting error: The metadata constraints '[Precision=0]' do not allow to format the value
entry.content:
Hi Folks,

we are using simple iFlow in CPI to create material document in SAP S4HANA Cloud public edition (2SL) with Odata service API_MATERIAL_DOCUMENT_SRV. We tried to change message mapping but it was failing so we reverted it back the previous version but we are still getting the same error:

com.sap.gateway.core.ip.component.odata.exception.ODataV2ResponseParsingException: Caused by:org.apache.olingo.odata2.api.edm.EdmSimpleTypeException: The metadata constraints '[Precision=0]' do not allow to format the value 'java.util.GregorianCalendar[time=17423067770,areFieldsSet=true,areAllFieldsSet=true,lenient=true,zone=sun.util.calendar.ZoneInfo[id="GMT",offset=0,dstSavings=0,useDaylight=false,transitions=0,lastRule=null],firstDayOfWeek=1,minimalDaysInFirstWeek=1,ERA=1,YEAR=1970,MONTH=6,WEEK_OF_YEAR=30,WEEK_OF_MONTH=4,DAY_OF_MONTH=21,DAY_OF_YEAR=202,DAY_OF_WEEK=3,DAY_OF_WEEK_IN_MONTH=3,AM_PM=1,HOUR=3,HOUR_OF_DAY=15,MINUTE=44,SECOND=27,MILLISECOND=770,ZONE_OFFSET=0,DST_OFFSET=0]'. in EntitySet A_MaterialDocumentHeaderThe metadata constraints '[Precision=0]' do not allow to format the value 'java.util.GregorianCalendar[time=17423067770,areFieldsSet=true,areAllFieldsSet=true,lenient=true,zone=sun.util.calendar.ZoneInfo[id="GMT",offset=0,dstSavings=0,useDaylight=false,transitions=0,l...

We tried to send date with milliseconds, without, only zeros and still failing.

It is failing after request reply to SAP S4HANA system.

We are sending:

{
    "GoodsMovementCode": "02",
    "MaterialDocument": "",
    "MaterialDocumentYear": "",
    "PostingDate": "/Date(17423067770)/"
}

Does anybody know how to fix this issue?

Thanks in advance for your help.

entry.title: Cloud Integration - JMS Queue - Entries Number
entry.content:
Dear experts.
I hope all is well with you.

We are evaluating the implementation of a solution to notify alerts when we notice X amount of entries in a JMS queue.
We tried checking CI's OData V2 API, Message Stores, JMS Resources (expanding to QueueStates), but it only shows whether the queue is healthy or not (based on number of entries vs capacity).
https://api.sap.com/api/MessageStore/path/get_JmsBrokers__Broker1__
Sample API Response:
{

"Name": "QueueName",
"State": "0"
}

It would be very valuable if this API had an entity/resource that returned the number of entries/messages in the queue.
 
Another possibility could be executing CI API (MessageProcessingLogs) to get the messages in the monitor. Apply the filter status + iFLow ID:

...CItenanthost/api/v1/MessageProcessingLogs/?$filter= Status eq 'RETRY' and IntegrationArtifact/Id eq 'IFLOW_ID'.

In an technical iFlow that will be executed periodically, executing the above query, if messages found, check the defined number of messages to send the alert, if it has been exceeded, send the email.

We are also checking the use of SAP ALM connected with Integration Suite Tenant, perhaps adding the SAP ANS (Alert Notification) BTP service to the solution.

Anyone from the community willing to contribute extra information, insights or who has worked on the same need?

I appreciate any feedback/comments.

Thank you,
Fellipe Mendes

#cloudintegration #ci #JMS #queue

entry.title: Hi Priyanka,Just like UPDATE_INSERT, Do SAP CPI supports DELETE_INSERT?
Automatic sending of SalesOrder messages from the S/4 HANA Public Cloud to SAP Event Mesh
entry.content:
Hello Community,

When a new SalesOrder message is created or updated in the S/4 HANA Public Cloud, I would like this message to be automatically triggered in the SAP Event Mesh and stored in the salesorder queue of the SAP Event Mash. I have already stored an “event mesh” system with the corresponding host and outbound communication (OAuth 2.0 via clientID and clientsecret from the event mesh) in the S/4 HANA Public Cloud under Communication Systems. I have created a sales order queue directly in the event mesh under the corresponding message client and stored the topic “sap/s4/beh/salesorder/created/v1”. What steps still need to be taken explicitly in the S/4 HANA Public Cloud so that the sales orders are automatically sent to the queue?

In general, I later retrieve the contents of the queue from the cloud integration via an AMQP adapter and then the other SalesOrder information using an OData call.

Thanks in advance and best regards

Michael Seydler

entry.title: Error in XI to JDBC Iflow
entry.content:
Hi,
In SAP CPI, I've XI to JDBC IFlow. PFA SS. At JDBC receiver I'm getting error: java.sql.SQLException: Incorrect syntax near ','.

Although some records are successfully received in the database, but others are failing with this error.
I am using the INSERT action on the DB. What could be the possible reason for this issue?

Detailed error:


Your help would be greatly appreciated!
